adds.rationale: Centrus Energy is the pure-play US uranium enrichment name; with Russian enrichment imports curtailed and hyperscaler-nuclear PPAs (AWS/Talen, Microsoft/Constellation, Google/Kairos, Amazon/X-energy) signed through 2024, domestic enrichment is the structural bottleneck the watchlist does not yet own.

news_evidence.summary: Nuclear ETFs hit new highs as AI data-center power demand pulls in uranium and enrichment names; Centrus called out as the essential US enricher after Russian-import sanctions.

NUKZ and peer nuclear ETFs broke out April 2025 on SMR approvals, IRA subsidies, and hyperscaler baseload demand, with NUKZ up ~100% from lows by Sept 2025 - confirms the buildup-to-surge transition of the nuclear wave the profile names.

removes.rationale: ARKQ (autonomous tech & robotics) overlaps heavily with BOTZ on robotics exposure and with SMH/NVDA on AI-autonomy holdings; removing the more diffuse generalist frees a slot for the pure-play enrichment name without losing thematic coverage, and the AI/autonomy stack is the bucket most exposed to the Q2-25 hyperscaler-capex overbuilding concern flagged by sell-side.

news_evidence.summary: Q2 2025 hyperscaler earnings: Alphabet revised 2025 capex to $91-93B, Microsoft $34.9B in a single quarter (+74% YoY), Amazon $125B 2025 guide, Meta $70B - some sell-side analysts flagged the pace as potential overbuild risk relative to ~$25B of 2025 AI-services revenue.

DeepSeek R1 release (Jan 27 2025) wiped ~$600B off Nvidia in a single session and reopened the debate on whether frontier-AI capex justifies the autonomy/robotics adjacencies ARKQ holds; thesis for the diffuse generalist ETF has weakened relative to focused exposures already in the watchlist.

rationale_overall: Swap a diffuse autonomous-tech ETF (ARKQ) for a pure-play US uranium enrichment name (LEU), tilting further toward the profile's near-term next-wave priority (nuclear / SMRs / hyperscaler PPAs).
